With their retirement together waiting on the sale of his house, Phil despairs of finding the right estate agent. The current one suggests an 'open day', but Phil baulks at the idea. Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. Queenie confesses to Edith that she can't bear the thought of her going away, and when she hears that plans are currently stalled by the difficulty of selling Phil's house, she sees her chance to influence events(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents are rocked by the news of Phil's attack, although some who know a little more than others begin to panic and try to cover the truth. Eventually, however, an arrest is made. But have the police got the real culprit? A mix-up over annoying loud music leaves Ruby racked with guilt, and inadvertently leads to an argument between Linda and Mick. Sheree makes herself a little too much at home, much to Denise's annoyance, and Callum struggles to write his wedding vows(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Stacey Meets the IS Brides - Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The presenters look back at some of our amazing dens from previous series(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: After encouraging Nish's disastrous tactics, Marnie rallies the troops as they face a hostile team(n)

programme.title: Deadly Top 10
programme.desc: Steve Backshall counts down the 10 creatures with the most developed senses, including killer taste buds and a crocodile with a motion-detecting jaw(n)

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: Zoologist George McGavin concludes his exploration of the primate world, considering their capacity to use tools and solve problems, something he claims sets them apart from most other families on the planet. By way of demonstration, the presenter goes to orang-utan school in Sumatra, visits Thailand to see long-tailed macaques floss their teeth with human hair and use tools to open oysters on the beach, and reveals how Ugandan chimpanzees pass on cultures and customs down the generations. He also meets a resourceful bonobo in the States that can order its own picnic on the phone and build a fire, on which it then toasts marshmallows(n)

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the first of two programmes in which they put their canine chums and feline friends under the microscope, with the aim of answering the age-old question - which are best, cats or dogs? The animals go head to head in a series of rounds testing different aspects, beginning in this edition with intelligence, sensory powers and physical prowess. On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris comes face to face with a pack of wolves, while feline fan Liz confronts an Arabian wild cat to discover how the relationship between humans and their pets have evolved(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary series following the ups and downs of six farming families in Scotland and Northern Ireland as they face up to tough economic times and an uncertain future. Crofting couple Lynn Cassells and Sandra Baer face the danger of a drought, while buffalo farmer Stevie Mitchell's herd go for a cooling dip in the loch. At the Royal Highland Show, hill farmer Johnny Irvine and his son Raymond are hoping for success with their Charolais cattle, while Aimee Budge from Shetland puts her pony skills to the test(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson go hunting for hidden corners of the UK landscape and meet some of the people who live there. They begin by exploring the unseen water world of the Brecon Beacons in south Wales, where Ellie confronts her fear of tight spaces to venture into Britain's deepest cave - carved through the rock by an ancient underground river - and a couple reveal how their lives were transformed when they bought a 200-year-old toll bridge over the River Wye. There are also visits to a mysterious pond known as the Witches' Pool and a magical walkway behind a waterfall(n)

programme.title: The Red Shoes
programme.desc: A budding prima ballerina is set to achieve her dream of stardom when she decides to leave her prestigious company to marry a composer - a decision that enrages her possessive mentor, who vows revenge. Powell and Pressburger's drama, based on the story by Hans Christian Andersen, starring Anton Walbrook, Moira Shearer, Marius Goring, Robert Helpmann and Albert Basserman(n)

credits.actor: Anton Walbrook, Moira Shearer, Marius Goring, Robert Helpmann, Albert Basserman

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin joins a research expedition across the Galapagos Islands, the vast archipelago where evolution has proceeded at an extraordinary pace. The journey begins on the west side, home to a richly diverse wildlife scene, where Liz and the scientists journey into the clouds above the islands' tallest volcano on a hunt for the endangered pink iguana. Liz also travels to one of the most remote locations in the Galapagos in search of giant tortoises, and boards a submersible to dive for a new species(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part one of two. The reasons why young animals enjoy playing, and how it can teach them to survive in the wild. The programme explores how young elephants, which rarely leave their mothers' side for the first eight years of life, play to develop social skills, while macaques hone their mental agility by playing games with stones. Plus, a zoologist who looks after orphaned cheetahs reveals how play can help cubs learn to hunt(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexico's best kept secret for fabulous wines(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the annual race, as the riders complete 3.4km circuits in central London. WNT-Rotor Pro Cycling Team's Kirsten Wild prevailed last year, with the Dutch rider managing to avoid a late crash to outsprint her rivals and become the first woman to win this race for a second time, having also been victorious in 2016. However, she is expected to face stiff competition this time around, as leading teams such as Boels-Dolmans, Trek-Segafredo and Team Sunweb are in attendance(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by Australian tenor Stuart Skelton and Finnish violinist Pekka Kuusisto for the Proms companion show. The Rev Richard Coles receives a tutorial from Notre Dame organist Olivier Latry and there is a piano and cello performance by sibling stars Sheku and Isata Kanneh-Mason(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and his platoon set up an observation post in the local lighthouse, but manage to annoy ARP warden Hodges by inadvertently flooding the whole town with light during an air raid. Comedy first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier(n)

credits.actor: Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In Kenya, new-born elephant calf Safina learns how to keep up with her fast moving herd, and in Sri Lanka, one-week-old Jazir has to quickly learn the ropes of a primate society. At the edge of the Arctic, five-week-old fox cubs must learn to hunt before their siblings beat them to limited food, and in Uganda, an eight-week-old mountain gorilla adapts to forest life(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profiling the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Part one of two. Celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Fern Brady, Desiree Burch, Alan Carr, Larry Dean, Eddie Izzard, Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Van and her friends score exclusive invitations to rapper Drake's New Year's Eve party and have big hopes of meeting the host. However, things do not turn out as planned. Comedy drama, starring Zazie Beetz(n)

credits.actor: Zazie Beetz

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: After getting into an argument with his girlfriend, Paper Boi storms off, gets mugged, gets lost in the woods, and meets a creepy old man who will not stop following and taunting him. Comedy drama, starring Brian Tyree Henry(n)

credits.actor: Brian Tyree Henry

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the event, which this year is staged at Woburn Golf Club in Buckinghamshire. The course last played host to this event in 2016 and saw Thailand's Ariya Jutanugarn claim victory. Georgia Hall won the 2018 tournament at Royal Lytham & St Annes, becoming the first English player to win the prestigious title since Karen Stupples in 2004(n)

programme.title: Heimat Home from Home: Chronicle of a Vision
programme.sub-title: Heimat Home from Home: Chronicle of a Vision - Part Two
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part prequel to the drama about three families. In 1883, a blacksmith's son dreams of a new life, while his brother returns from military service. In German(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden's balloon travels over the flattest part of Britain as she descends on Norfolk. She helps cut a hole in a hedge at East Ruston Gardens, assists in preparing a tree for felling at Bressingham Gardens, and discovers the human history of the Norfolk Broads(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don catches up on jobs to keep the garden looking good all summer long. Adam Frost visits Sledmere Gardens in East Yorkshire where the borders have been planted with specific colour schemes in mind. The programme also catches up with a family in the Vale of Glamorgan whose members pack their small garden with colourful bedding plants for maximum impact(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Anita Rani head to Gloucestershire to celebrate the 50th anniversary of the Riding for the Disabled Association, attending its national championships at Hartpury College, while Princess Anne talks about her long association with the organisation. Sean Fletcher learns how to make a canoe out of flax, Adam Henson helps with a cherry harvest in Herefordshire and Tom Heap looks at the threat posed by equine flu(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George Anderson finds out how Saughton Park in Edinburgh has been regenerated since he visited in 2017 and Chris Beardshaw demonstrates creative and fun ways of pruning(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: The food writer shows people who lack confidence in the kitchen how to pull off an impressive meal for friends and family, demonstrating plenty of deceptively easy recipes and offering invaluable culinary tips. In the first episode, she helps 52-year-old builder Tony - who has never cooked a meal in his life - to master a delicious Mediterranean-inspired menu, including a grilled peach and burrata salad, crispy salmon with polenta chips and grilled asparagus, as well as a showstopping dessert(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Four food producers with fledgling businesses test out their products on the locals in Malhamdale in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. They have two days in the shop to promote and sell their wares. This time it's makers of preserves, pickles and spreads battling it out for a place in the final. Tom Kerridge meets members of the community as they try the unusual preserves. Meanwhile, judges Alison Swan Parente and Nisha Katona visit the food producers at home to see how well their product is made(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding looks ahead to today's cycling in London and showcases the behind-the-scenes stories of amateur participants raising money for charity(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Diane Louise Jordan on a trip to her childhood home of Hatfield, Hertfordshire, where she remembers her mother's Caribbean cooking and school lunches(n)

programme.title: Oklahoma!
programme.desc: A farm girl is torn between two very different suitors - a charming cowboy wandering through the territory and a brooding farmhand with a dark side to his nature. Meanwhile, another cattle driver has finally raised enough money to marry his sweetheart, only to find he has a rival. Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical Western, with Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones and Rod Steiger. Songs include Oh What a Beautiful Mornin' and I Cain't Say No(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at Mashpi Lodge in Ecuador, which is 900 metres above sea level and features a gondola cable car that transports guests a mile through the rainforest canopy. The pair reveal the challenges of delivering the highest standards of experience in the midst of teeming wildlife, mud and moisture, as well as the eco-tourism that is saving thousands of species, and also spend time with their new co-workers at home, hearing moving stories of how local people's lives have been transformed by the arrival of the hotel(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Anita Rani and Ade Adepitan go behind-the-scenes to reveal the systems and people running the Indian city that has a population of 26 million people and rising, and which contains the country's capital. Anita visits Asia's largest fruit and vegetable market to discover how this ever-growing city continues to feed itself, while Ade braves the back streets to get a taste of life as a porter in one of India's oldest markets. Elsewhere, Dan explores how the city has grown from its origins at the heart of the great Mughal Empire to capital of the world's largest democracy(n)

programme.title: Mediterranean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The final leg of the journey takes in Morocco, where Simon visits a land borders between Africa and the European Union, and the coast of Spain, taking in the scale of the country's fruit and vegetable industry. He discovers why tourists are actively discouraged from visiting Corsica, and goes diving in waters that have the highest fish population in the Mediterranean(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian travels to Mongolia, the most sparsely populated country on Earth. With the aid of a local TV host, he spends a week exploring the vast nation. He tries his hand at traditional wrestling and shamanic rituals, encounters an enormous statue of Genghis Khan, takes part in a hunting trip with eagles, and visits an energy centre in the middle of the desert. He also discovers how relationships are formed among nomadic societies, where people often live more than 100 miles away from anyone else(n)

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Spencer Jones as a man trying to navigate modern life with constant interruptions from his own bizarre imagination, and who struggles to balance his career as a performer with his everyday responsibilities. In the first episode, Herbert's best friend Jonny performs a remarkable impression of his pet dog(n)

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Herbert is given the opportunity of a lifetime when he is asked to record an audition tape for a role in a musical. He decides that the performance most likely to win him the role is a rendition of a 1980s computer struggling to load(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux's Altered States
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux's Altered States: Love without Limits
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores the changing face of US culture, beginning with a look at a movement in Portland, Oregon, that seeks to broaden how the world views marriage in a time of rising divorce rates, suggesting that monogamy is not essential and that polyamorous or open relationships can be equally fulfilling. However, while Louis meets many who have found happiness with multiple partners, he also discovers tales of heartbreak and jealousy(n)

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the event, which this year was staged at Woburn Golf Club in Buckinghamshire. England's Georgia Hall was the victorious player last year at Royal Lytham and St Anne's, beginning the concluding round in second place, but shooting a round of four-under to finish two strokes ahead of Thailand's Pornanong Phatlum(n)

programme.title: How the Middle Class Ruined Britain
programme.desc: Working-class Tory and leave-voting comedian Geoff Norcott sets out on a mission to expose the middle-class hypocrisy that he believes is ruining Britain, looking at education, the gentrification of parts of big cities, and the worlds of dating and politics(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The team gives a warm send-off to a beloved colleague, but a surprising revelation leaves Cameron reeling and he soon finds himself drunk and out of control on the wards. Under Lofty's guidance, Dominic takes steps to reconcile with Carole, only to end up making things even worse between them. Chloe is determined to sever all contact with Evan, but is forced to seek him out when he goes missing(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of the British Garden
programme.desc: The extraordinary changes that occurred in the 19th century, as advances in technology made gardens more lavish, but also more widely available. Monty pays a visit to Queen Victoria's royal retreat at Osborne and Derby's Arboretum, which was the country's first public park. He also investigates the scientific secrets behind the establishment of botanical gardens in Kew and Edinburgh(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: The islanders receive an update on the oil exploration going on offshore. While many residents are excited about the possible benefits such a commodity could bring, others are concerned about the inevitable changes that will occur. Elsewhere, resident marine biologist Megan Tierney pioneers some research using a tracking system, and sets out to catch some Gentoo penguins so that she can find out where they go to feed during the winter. Meanwhile, the islanders practise their dance steps for the annual May Ball(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the gardeners of Marlborough in Wiltshire. They won gold last year and are hoping to retain that coveted award by transforming one of the town's ancient alleyways and also by brightening up a community corner by the river(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the capital on his rail journey from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In Ealing, he is transformed for the silver screen by expert hair and make-up, before travelling to Fulham, where he heads underground to explore London's new super sewer. Then, Michael visits the London School of Economics and Political Science in central London, before finally arriving in Kensington(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Christy Harrison and Vim Kumaratunga for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Nazan wants her kitchen in Wokingham refreshing on a budget of £3,000, while in Wigan, Chris and Joanna need the balustrade on their staircase replacing(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott takes Len Goodman on a road trip around Britain to expand the Strictly Come Dancing judge's culinary horizons. The pair begin in Manchester, where Len has his first taste of Chinese food and samples some Mexican mezcal, while Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a stir-fried beef dish inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Farm vet Richard visits a long-standing client, where his work involves anything from de-horning a cow to trimming an overgrown hoof, rounded off with tea and home-made scones. Meanwhile, Megan treats a seriously ill Yorkshire terrier at the Small Animal Hospital in Fife, and there is news for an unsuspecting cat owner when vet Adam makes a surprise discovery. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough documents the universal tale that unites humanity with every other animal on the planet - the journey through life, and the goal to continue the bloodline through offspring. This edition focuses on the first hurdle any animal will face, surviving infancy. Cameras follow flightless barnacle goose chicks as they strive to find food, leaping 400ft down a cliff from the ledge where they hatched. Meanwhile, in New Zealand, young fur seals have stumbled across a safe haven in which they can learn how to avoid predators, while deep in the Gobi Desert, the long-eared jerboa learns to use its impeccable hearing to catch insect pray. Plus, albatross chicks make their first flight, as threatening tiger sharks wait patiently for them to slip up and land in the sea(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: James Braxton and Arusha Irvine brave the elements in their search for bargains, heading through the snow from Northumberland to Edinburgh in their '66 Mustang(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts a celebrity edition of the show, as string quartet Bond and composer David Arnold take on the winners of famous game shows for a chance to win money for charity(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix to some of his favourite restaurants in Paris. First, the pair visit one of the most famous restaurants in the city, La Tour D'Argent, where they experience a signature dish that has been on the menu since 1890. Moving away from restaurants that offer expensive indulgence, Michel takes Fred to a place reviving one of the simplest and cheapest French classics - the ham baguette. For the final stop of their Parisian trip, the pair visit Brasserie La Couple, which boasts the biggest dining room in Paris(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates recipes to take the stress out of impressing guests, including raspberry cheesecake croissants, a speedy satay chicken tray bake and a fuss-free cheat's trifle with creamy chia seeds, yoghurt, fresh fruit and sweet brioche. In Walsall, Nadiya's shortcut curry with spicy jackfruit and speedy naan gives a novice cook the family dinner party that she's always dreamed of(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the third of the student quiz's first-round matches, four students from the University of Birmingham pit their wits against a team from the University of Bristol in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Conjoined Twins: An Impossible Decision
programme.desc: Conjoined twins Marieme and Ndeye have separate hearts and lungs, but Marieme's heart is very weak and her life expectancy is poor and if she dies, her stronger sister will die with her. As the girls' health deteriorates, their father Ibrahima and staff at Great Ormond Street Hospital are forced to decide whether a separation should be considered. This programme tracks the journey of Ibrahima and the medical team as they wrestle with is a seemingly impossible decision with the help of the hospital's Clinical Ethics Committee(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Grace Jones presents a look back at a year when Live Aid took the world by storm, Prince and Bruce Springsteen topped the charts, and Miami Vice set the standard in men's fashion. Anarchic comic Viz hit newsagents' shelves nationwide and the second series of Spitting Image poked fun at the likes of Roy Hattersley and David Steel, who discuss how it felt to be on the receiving end(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan travels around the world, learning traditional hunting methods from members of tribal cultures and joining them in pursuing dangerous predators(n)

programme.title: The Last Seabird Summer?
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adam Nicolson catches up with the seabirds of the Shiants in July, and the colonies seem to be thriving. The puffings guillemots and razorbills are bringing in bill-fuls of fish to feed their chicks, but he discovers this scene is not a common one. The sea is failing to provide the birds with the food they need to survive, and in Scotland alone, an estimated 40 per cent of seabirds have been lost. Some olonies in Iceland are reported to have been wiped out, and Adam speaks to puffin hunters to discover how they have responded to this problem. Plus, with the help of expert ornithologists and marine scientists, he gains an insight into the global forces driving the crisis(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the Antipodean island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Now on extended leave from the police, Philip Norgaard is contacted by June al-Baqee, a Danish national who has been prosecuted for fighting against ISIS in Syria, who says she has evidence of Danish involvement in the Syrian War. However, June is kidnapped as she waits for the detective. Return of the thriller, starring Johannes Lassen(n)

credits.actor: Johannes Lassen

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Philip follows June's trail to the ferry between Denmark and Sweden and warns the captain that a kidnapping is taking place onboard and that he should activate the silent alarm. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Merely Marvelous - The Dancing Genius of Gwen Verdon
programme.desc: A celebration of the art and life of the Broadway star, who overcame obstacles including rickets, the Hollywood system and a difficult marriage to choreographer and director Bob Fosse to become a multi-Tony Award winning performer. Verdon's life is told through interviews with family members and theatre associates as well rare footage from her stage and screen career(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Up-close and personal examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words from hours of archived interviews, along with commentary from those closest to him. Featuring previously unseen footage from home movies and concert performances, including his legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles, this tribute to the showman follows Sinatra's growth from roadhouse performer to global singing sensation(n)

programme.title: Can You Feel It – How Dance Music Conquered the World
programme.desc: A celebration of club culture, looking at the pioneering sound systems that powered the clubs that transitioned disco into house, and the multibillion dollar business that is modern clubbing. Plus, New Order members Stephen Morris and Bernard Sumner recall the New York trip that led to the birth of the Manchester dance mecca Hacienda(n)

programme.title: REM at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances from throughout the band's career, originally recorded for BBC programmes including The Old Grey Whistle Test, Top of the Pops, Parkinson, The Late Show and Later with Jools Holland. Songs featured include Losing My Religion, Orange Crush, Pretty Persuasion and Half a World Away. Singer Michael Stipe and bass player Mike Mills also reflect on the band's split in September 2011(n)

programme.title: Size Matters
programme.desc: Hannah Fry presents the first of two documentaries in which she looks at the science of size. She begins by imagining what life on Earth would be like if the planet were much bigger than it already is, learning about the biggest ape to ever exist and imagining what a 15m tall human body would look like(n)

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Portrait of artist Peter Blake, who has enjoyed a celebrated career lasting over 60 years and is famous for his Beatles' Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band album cover design. This film follows him at work in his studio and follows the process as he undertakes his largest work to date when he was commissioned, at the age of 82, to re-design a Mersey ferry and give it a Pop Art makeover(n)

programme.title: Brighton: 50 Years of Gay
programme.desc: Simon Fanshawe marks the 50th anniversary of the Sexual Offences Act 1967, which which decriminalised private homosexual acts between men aged over 21. The writer and broadcaster examines this landmark change in the law and reveals the story of the fight for gay equality through the colourful history of his home town of Brighton(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: John Storgards conducts the BBC Philharmonic in two dark Russian classics, Rachmaninov's atmospheric The Isle of the Dead and Shostakovich's broody Symphony No 11, The Year 1905. Bringing light and hope is the world premiere of Finnish composer Outi Tarkiainen's Midnight Sun Variations. Introduced by Tom Service and Jess Gillam from the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Grammy winner Angelique Kidjo makes her Proms debut at the Royal Albert Hall with her nine-piece band in a tribute to salsa star Celia Cruz. Clara Amfo explores the links between the music of Cuban diva, Cruz and the traditional Yoruba music that came to America during the slave trade, and how Kidjo has combined these influences with the West-African Afrobeat and juju styles(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Artist Stanley Spencer's daughters Unity and Shirin discuss their unusual family life. They discuss how they became estranged from each other following their father's traumatic divorce and failed second marriage, and have only recently been reunited with each other in their old age. The film follows them as they box up their father's drawings and letters while preparing to live together(n)

programme.title: Can You Feel It – How Dance Music Conquered the World
programme.desc: How DJs moved from invisibility to centre-stage and celebrity, becoming the highest-paid stars in today's music industry and influencing the music modern society listens to. The programme follows Greg Wilson demonstrating the novel act of mixing two records on a 1980s edition of The Tube, through Ibiza vibe-pioneer Alfredo, to Paul Oakenfold's legendary sets at acid-house night Spectrum. Contributors include Pete Tong, Fabio, David Guetta, Steve Aoki, Tiesto, Paul Van Dyk, Black Madonna, Moby and Midland. Narrated by Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Portrait of artist Peter Blake, who has enjoyed a celebrated career lasting over 60 years and is famous for his Beatles' Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band album cover design. This film follows him at work in his studio and follows the process as he undertakes his largest work to date when he was commissioned, at the age of 82, to re-design a Mersey ferry and give it a Pop Art makeover(n)

programme.title: Brighton: 50 Years of Gay
programme.desc: Simon Fanshawe marks the 50th anniversary of the Sexual Offences Act 1967, which which decriminalised private homosexual acts between men aged over 21. The writer and broadcaster examines this landmark change in the law and reveals the story of the fight for gay equality through the colourful history of his home town of Brighton(n)

programme.title: Size Matters
programme.desc: Hannah Fry imagines a world in which things are smaller. She finds out what would happen if the Earth was half the size it is now, shrinks people to just 5mm tall and finds out the minimum size of the sun and other stars(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: Artists arrive for the Biennale arts festival, but the grand scale of some of the exhibits puts a strain on the city's infrastructure. Anish Kapoor tries to stage his installation Ascension at the Basilica di San Giorgio, while another artist struggles to transport a four-ton sculpture by canal. The Fenice theatre undergoes a vital safety check, and a cruise liner sails dangerously close to the city centre(n)

programme.title: Arena: Philip Hoare's Guide to Whales
programme.desc: Acclaimed author and whale-watcher Philip Hoare explores the worlds of three types of whale, beginning with baleen whales, the largest animals ever to have lived(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past(n)

programme.title: James May's Toy Stories
programme.sub-title: James May's Toy Stories: Action Man
programme.desc: For decades, Action Man has promised much with its eagle eyes, gripping hands and array of outfits, but what has he ever achieved? James May has decided he is going to try to redeem its reputation by propelling one of the figures through the sound barrier and return it safely to Earth. James and his team have to deal with high-speed physics, unforeseen explosions and catastrophic design setbacks, before settling on an experimental supersonic vehicle made entirely from scratch. Finally, Action Man must go faster and higher than another attempt led by arch-rival Sindy(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: An insight into mammals' ability to learn new tricks to aid hunting prey and evading predators. Slow-motion cameras reveal the star-nosed mole's technique for detecting its next meal underwater, while an ibex runs up a steep cliff face to escape a fox. A killer whale off the coast of the Falklands sneaks into a pool used by elephant seal pups and snatches them, saving itself the trouble of searching for food in the open sea, and three cheetah brothers work together to hunt a zebra. Narrated by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Pedalling Dreams: The Raleigh Story
programme.desc: Documentary telling the tale of the ups and downs of Raleigh bikes, using personal testimony and rare and previously unseen archive film. Born in the back streets of Nottingham in 1888, Raleigh grew to become the biggest bicycle manufacturer in the world and, for more than a century, was known for its simple and practical bikes(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Rattles' mother starts dating Archie Vanderloo, and he must find something in common with her new beau's children(n)

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The farmer throws a fancy-dress party, but Bitzer accidentally loses all the invitations - so Shaun saves the day by asking the flock to masquerade as guests(n)

programme.desc: Penfold takes a trip inside Danger Mouse's body to save him from a deadly aversion to his own heroism(n)

programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life(n)

programme.desc: The A-Troupe is reunited after their win at regionals, while Emily and Michelle set aside their differences over Eldon(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: The A-Troupe meets their new choreographer and votes for open auditions for the Nationals Team, with many of the Troupers and some unexpected newcomers attending(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: The first round of auditions for the national team takes place, and Phoebe, the new head choreographer, challenges the dancers in the final round(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: The announcement of the Nationals team brings disappointment for some, and Hunter enlists Emily's help to make Michelle jealous(n)

programme.title: Gym Stars
programme.desc: The rhythmic team members have a fight on their hands when a long-term member quits and the team's reserve steps in to try to save the day. For the first time ever, the Acrobatic Men's Four are invited to trials for the World Championships. However, when it comes to costumes for the day, they realise their performance on the floor is not their only concern! And Olympic and Commonwealth Champion Nile Wilson sets what for most, is an impossible challenge(n)

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Dr Xand investigates who stole his strawberry milk and finds out more about DNA(n)

programme.desc: With the help of opera singer Lucy O'Bryne, doctors Chris and Dr Xand van Tulleken reveal the hidden muscle that makes people breathe. Dr Xand heads to a DNA laboratory to solve the mystery of who stole his strawberry milk, and there's a chance to play along at home with another mind-bending trick. Meanwhile, the 'Ouch Mobile' is open for business, and over in Accident & Emergency one patient has cut their eyebrow, and another has stapled his own finger(n)

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A selection of monarchs, emperors and political leaders from the series, including naughty Napoleon annoying his Josephine, and horrid Henry VIII getting married - again. Plus, a song from wily Winston Churchill. Starring Rowan Atkinson, Kevin Eldon, Ben Miller and Tom Rosenthal(n)

programme.sub-title: Hamza,Truc and Anushka: Truc has a little sleep - at school
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of ordinary children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having a very busy day. In Jordan, seven-year-old Hamza is having trouble getting up for school. But will he get there in time? At school in Vietnam, Truc, who's seven, plays lots of games, has a tasty lunch and a little sleep with her friends. And after school in India, six-year-old Anushka learns how to draw patterns on the ground called Rangoli with her aunt. In each film we see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal(n)

Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway's walk in Cardigan Bay has something for everyone. For the first half he's at one with nature as he explores one of Britain's largest and oldest raised peat bogs. His route then takes him to a unique railway museum, filled with nostalgia and he also discovers the legend of Cantre Gwaelod at Sarn Cynfelyn where the shingle spit stretches an incredible 11kms out to sea. He finishes at the university town of Aberystwyth with its Victorian cliff-top pleasure park, complete with camera obscura. Derek also has a go at paddle-boarding - which has its challenges(n)

programme.sub-title: White Van Gogh Man - Our Lives

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents are rocked by the news of Phil's attack, although some who know a little more than others begin to panic and try to cover the truth. Eventually, however, an arrest is made. But have the police got the real culprit? A mix-up over annoying loud music leaves Ruby racked with guilt, and inadvertently leads to an argument between Linda and Mick. Sheree makes herself a little too much at home, much to Denise's annoyance, and Callum struggles to write his wedding vows(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Stacey Meets the IS Brides - Panorama
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

programme.desc: In Winchester, Hampshire, busy doctor Carolyn wants a tranquil spot to help her escape the stresses of her high-pressured job. It'll be a tough challenge however, as there are very few living plants in her garden to start with(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: The late-in-life comedy starring Alison Steadman and John Cleese returns for its second run. With their retirement together waiting on the sale of his house, Phil despairs of finding the right estate agent. The current one suggests an 'open day', but Phil baulks at the idea. Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: A hero is needed to defend a peaceful valley community from attack by a rampaging snow leopard. The unlikely candidate for the job is a lazy panda, who enlists the aid of a legendary team of animal fighters and their wise master to teach him the martial arts skills he needs to fulfil his destiny. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jack Black, Dustin Hoffman, Angelina Jolie, Ian McShane and Jackie Chan(n)

programme.desc: The late-in-life comedy starring Alison Steadman and John Cleese returns for its second run. With their retirement together waiting on the sale of his house, Phil despairs of finding the right estate agent. The current one suggests an 'open day', but Phil baulks at the idea. Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. Queenie confesses to Edith that she can't bear the thought of her going away, and when she hears that plans are currently stalled by the difficulty of selling Phil's house, she sees her chance to influence events(n)

The eight women and four men, including three grandmothers, a fire officer, a police inspector, a builder, a veterinary nurse, a foster carer and a student, all pull together for nearly 100 miles of open sea that can take over a day to cross. programme.desc: Stacey Dooley travels to holding camps in Kurdish-controlled northern Syria, where she meets Western women who left their home countries to join Islamic State, and also finds the camps filled with European children. The Kurds in control of this part of Syria say the Western powers should not expect them to be jailers on their behalf indefinitely, and Stacey explores the question of what to do with these women and children who have been left behind(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In a first for the series, two related celebrities join forces to investigate their family tree as comedian and actor Jack Whitehall and his father and occasional double-act partner and talk show co-host Michael team up to look into their past. They learn about a tragedy that left Michael's grandfather an orphan, and also follow their lineage back to Wales in the 1830s, where they find out about their Tory ancestor and his role fighting against the Chartist political and social reform movement(n)

The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Britain
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury explores the wonder of the Industrial Revolution, visiting the Pontcysyllte aqueduct, which carries the Llangollen canal over the valley of the River Dee near Wrexham. Completed in 1805, it is the longest and highest aqueduct in Britain and is now categorised as a Grade I listed building. She also boards Brunel's former passenger steamship the SS Great Britain and appreciates the gothic majesty of Manchester Town Hall and the iconic fun of Blackpool Tower(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a seemingly beloved resident of a coastal community is fatally driven off the road, and realises she has to dig up the victim's past to have any chance of unmasking the killer. Joined by her faithful team, including DC Kenny Lockhart and new recruit DC Jac Williams, Vera attempts to find the missing link that will crack the case, but finds it difficult to get to the truth when everyone they encounter seems to be protecting a secret. Crime drama based on Ann Cleeves' series of novels, starring Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the only prison on the Isle of Man, which houses 150 inmates and is governed an entirely different legal system from the rest of the UK. The first episode introduces the man who runs the prison shop, which inmates can use depending on their behaviour, and a couple who have both been locked up - one of whom is preparing to defend himself in his retrial. Elsewhere, cameras follow Ben, an inmate who has missed his grandmother's funeral whilst being in jail, as he receives a special visit by a member of staff who have agreed to bring her ashes to him(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: Chloe struggles to check in a passenger with a motorised wheelchair that could contain a potentially lethal battery. Singer Sinitta has a surprise for a couple of passengers - but Nathan has his hands full escorting her through the airport. A heavy landing leaves the door of a plane's hold blocked by hundreds of pieces of luggage(n)

programme.title: Counting Tigers: A Survival Special
programme.desc: Documentary examining a wildlife survey being conducted to take stock of India's tiger population, the results of which could be monumentally important to the animal's future. The camera crew is given unprecedented access to follow the survey across 50 tiger reserves and 400,000km in search of one of the most elusive predators on Earth. Narrated by Joanna Lumley(n)

programme.title: Rescue: River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Ullswater in the Lake District, the Patterdale Mountain Rescue team is called to assist a man who has broken his leg on the far shore of the lake, the second longest in the UK. Thankfully, the team has a high-speed boat in which to rush to his aid. In the Gap of Dunloe County in Kerry, a 22-year-old climber has fallen 75ft and hit a boulder. He has regained consciousness but has broken several bones and his pelvis. The Kerry Mountain Rescue Team, National Ambulance service and Irish Coast Guard helicopter winch him 200ft on a stretcher to the hovering helicopter(n)

Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the only prison on the Isle of Man, which houses 150 inmates and is governed an entirely different legal system from the rest of the UK. The first episode introduces the man who runs the prison shop, which inmates can use depending on their behaviour, and a couple who have both been locked up - one of whom is preparing to defend himself in his retrial. Elsewhere, cameras follow Ben, an inmate who has missed his grandmother's funeral whilst being in jail, as he receives a special visit by a member of staff who have agreed to bring her ashes to him(n)

programme.title: Counting Tigers: A Survival Special
programme.desc: Documentary examining a wildlife survey being conducted to take stock of India's tiger population, the results of which could be monumentally important to the animal's future. The camera crew is given unprecedented access to follow the survey across 50 tiger reserves and 400,000km in search of one of the most elusive predators on Earth. Narrated by Joanna Lumley(n)

programme.title: Rescue: River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Ullswater in the Lake District, the Patterdale Mountain Rescue team is called to assist a man who has broken his leg on the far shore of the lake, the second longest in the UK. Thankfully, the team has a high-speed boat in which to rush to his aid. In the Gap of Dunloe County in Kerry, a 22-year-old climber has fallen 75ft and hit a boulder. He has regained consciousness but has broken several bones and his pelvis. The Kerry Mountain Rescue Team, National Ambulance service and Irish Coast Guard helicopter winch him 200ft on a stretcher to the hovering helicopter(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

credits.actor: Julia McKenzie, a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer, Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star(n)

credits.actor: Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks co-

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter(n)

credits.actor: John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Rik Mayall, Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A mysterious fire that killed an artist with links to political activists leaves the police baffled, while Bright finds himself short-staffed when a stomach bug puts most of his men on sick leave. The situation becomes more serious when a housewife dies of the same condition. Endeavour investigates a family-run supermarket, believing the infection may have stemmed from their stock being deliberately contaminated, and discovers dark secrets that may have a connection to the unexplained blaze(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A routine visit turns into a nightmare for Jill when she is taken hostage by a psychologically damaged ex-prisoner of war, who has barricaded himself into his house and booby-trapped the surrounding land with mines. Meanwhile, Harper is spotted paying frequent visits to a woman in town and Alun is suspended from duty when his noisy band practice causes a woman to slip and break her hip. Medical drama, starring Robert Daws, Amy Robbins, Wendy Craig, Ian Carmichael, Michael Starke, John Axon and Andy Wear(n)

credits.actor: Robert Daws, Amy Robbins, Wendy Craig, Ian Carmichael, Michael Starke, John Axon, Andy Wear

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick is called out to a remote farmhouse, where she discovers three children have been left orphaned by the death of their father. Adamant that the family should stay together, Steve helps the eldest fight a custody battle, but a series of obstacles stands in their way. Meanwhile, Vernon opens a caravan park, but it is David who ends up having to entertain the not-so-happy campers(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve does his bit for the community by taking a group of delinquents on an outdoor adventure course on the moors, but one of the lads falls and breaks his ankle, while another is wanted for stealing from a shop. Vernon sees a money-making opportunity when a religious sect arrives in Aidensfield looking for somewhere to hold a meditation convention. Rural police drama, starring James Carlton and Geoffrey Hughes(n)

credits.actor: James Carlton, Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

credits.actor: Julia McKenzie, a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones, Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong, Edward Fox

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head for rural Cornwall, where they create a spectacular contemporary garden for Chris Finney, a former serviceman who was awarded the George Cross in 2003. A space age-inspired area - complete with solar dome - will test Chris's knowledge as a novice gardener, and provide a stimulating haven for his young family(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Chessington to create a car-themed garden for hospital worker and automobile fan Alan Rendall. In July 2014, he managed to rescue his unconscious neighbour, Roy Whiteway, from his burning house, but was unable to save Dorothy, Roy's wife, as the fire was so intense. To recognise his bravery, Alan and the team surprise the whole Rendall family and turn their featureless plot into an automobile-inspired haven(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately(n)

credits.actor: Ian McDiarmid, John Thaw, Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker, Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz(n)

credits.actor: Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Five-week-old puppy Domino refuses to stop biting, so Paul O'Grady pairs him with the older and wiser Freddie, but it won't be for long, as Freddie soon finds a new home in Denmark. Elsewhere, neglected shih-tzu Gizmo is in desperate need of a clean, and Battersea staff suspect nine-month-old Duchess may be pregnant(n)

programme.title: Tonight at the Palladium
programme.desc: Host Bradley Walsh welcomes soul and pop band Simply Red, French electro swing band Caravan Palace, singer-songwriter Pixie Lott, and the cast of the musical Charlie and the Chocolate Factory to the Palladium stage. Plus, there is entertainment by comic Dane Baptiste, Britain's Got Talent winners Attraction, and speciality act Naked Lunch, and singer Peter Andre may finally get to perform on the world-famous stage(n)

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky is left alone in the world and living in greatly reduced circumstances when Steyne arranges for Rawdon to be shipped overseas. Dobbin discovers that Amelia plans to remarry, a revelation that inspires him to finally confess his feelings for her - but he is not prepared for her reaction. The Sedley family takes a holiday in Germany, where they are unexpectedly reunited with Becky - who gets the chance to redeem a lifetime of selfishness by performing a single good deed on behalf of her dearest friend(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. John Nettles stars, with Rosalie Crutchley, Jonathan Firth and Emily Mortimer(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.desc: Rachel tries to persuade Mark to take his exam. Carol is late for work again. Eric tries to explain to Michael that he loves Elizabeth(n)

programme.desc: The school tells Joe that Mark did not turn up for his last exam. Seth catches Lorraine at the fish farm trying to steal some trout. Eric has a surprise for Elizabeth(n)

programme.desc: Gail is impressed when Martin gets a tax rebate, Bet is concerned as Jack is too tired to work properly. Curly advertises for a male lodger(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: An exciting offer for Edith divides opinion, Isobel throws Ethel a lifeline and Anna's perseverance finally pays off. Costume drama, starring Laura Carmichael, Penelope Wilton, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Dan Stevens(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in Jessica's garage leads to all manner of accusations, prompting her to embark on a determined quest to unmask the killer. Angela Lansbury stars alongside Ron Masak(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New neighbours look set to disturb the tranquillity of Portwenn, upsetting the locals with their unconventional lifestyle and even managing to gatecrash an intimate dinner between Martin and Louisa. Meanwhile, a car vandal strikes the village - but agoraphobic village copper PC Penhale is too scared to leave the station and investigate. Martin Clunes stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa faces off with love rival Carrie in a battle for Martin's affections, and romance is in the air for Aunt Joan, who gains an admirer in the form of Edward. Elsewhere, Al makes a surprise reappearance and PC Penhale celebrates his birthday. Martin Clunes stars, with Joe Absolom and Louise Delamere(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Crime drama following the personal and professional lives of two detectives working for a Manchester police unit that specialises in murder cases. DC Janet Scott (Lesley Sharp) and DC Rachel Bailey (Suranne Jones) investigate the case of a pregnant woman who was killed in her bedroom. credits.actor: Rupert Graves, Amelia Bullmore, Nicholas Gleaves

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet reopens the investigation into the murder of childhood friend Veronica, Rachel receives news that makes her personal life even more complicated, and a teenage murderer is successfully charged, but horrific events start to unravel following his arrest. credits.actor: Sally Lindsay, Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones

programme.desc: Stan and Jack are challenged to a darts match by canny clippies Iris and Jenny - who compensate for a lack of skill by using their feminine charms to gain an unfair advantage(n)

credits.actor: John Axon, Linda Armstrong, Michelle Hardwick

programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

credits.actor: Brian Murphy, Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. credits.actor: John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak, Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

credits.actor: Julia McKenzie, a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer, Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star(n)

Plus, there is entertainment by comic Dane Baptiste, Britain's Got Talent winners Attraction, and speciality act Naked Lunch, and singer Peter Andre may finally get to perform on the world-famous stage(n)

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky is left alone in the world and living in greatly reduced circumstances when Steyne arranges for Rawdon to be shipped overseas. Dobbin discovers that Amelia plans to remarry, a revelation that inspires him to finally confess his feelings for her - but he is not prepared for her reaction. The Sedley family takes a holiday in Germany, where they are unexpectedly reunited with Becky - who gets the chance to redeem a lifetime of selfishness by performing a single good deed on behalf of her dearest friend(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. John Nettles stars, with Rosalie Crutchley, Jonathan Firth and Emily Mortimer(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker, Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster journeys along the Trent and Mersey in the Midlands as his exploration of some of Britain's best canals and their history continues. As he travels through the Potteries, managing to smash his fair share of ceramics, he encounters a frightening, ghostly tunnel and takes up the challenge of a blind beer-tasting competition(n)

programme.desc: Jack hatches a plan to help Michael get his ban from the Woolpack lifted, and Eric decides to do something about improving his relationship with Michael(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit.
